title: laurembergia coccinea var. zeylanica
intro: Laurembergia coccinea var. zeylanica, also known as Ceylon tea or Red laurel, is a plant species that belongs to the family Phyllanthaceae and is native to Sri Lanka. The medicinal properties of Laurembergia coccinea var. zeylanica include its ability to act as a diuretic, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to help manage kidney stones, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. In traditional medicine, Laurembergia coccinea var. zeylanica is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it can also be used in herbal remedies, such as decoctions and tinctures.
